_source: Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) India — SME Cluster Energy Benchmarks
_references: BEE-SME Programme: Nagaur Hand Tools cluster, Jalandhar Hand Tools cluster (BEE-GEF-UNIDO)
BEE-SME Programme: SPM (Special Purpose Machines) — turning/drilling installed at Ludhiana forging units
BEE-GEF-WB Programme: Bangalore Machine Tools cluster (Annexure-1)
UNIDO Industrial Energy Efficiency — Machining and Metal Cutting benchmarks
BEE National Programme on EE in SMEs: 375 DPRs including machining sector clusters
_units: kWh per tonne of finished product
_notes: Machining SEC varies enormously by material removal rate and part complexity. These benchmarks represent typical MSME cluster operations (batch production, general-purpose or special-purpose machines). SPM (Special Purpose Machines) installed at Ludhiana forging units showed 20-40% energy savings vs conventional. Nagaur and Jalandhar hand tools clusters covered under BEE-GEF-UNIDO programme.

mild_steel_turning.notes: CNC/SPM turning of mild steel shafts, sleeves, bushings. SPM installed at Ludhiana forging cluster (BEE-SME). Yield loss from swarf/chips typically 20-30%.

mild_steel_milling.notes: Face milling, slot milling on steel components. Higher SEC than turning due to interrupted cut.

mild_steel_grinding.notes: Surface and cylindrical grinding. High specific energy (material removal is small per pass). Low material loss but high energy per kg removed.

mild_steel_drilling.notes: Drilling and boring operations. SPM-drilling installed at Bharat International, Ludhiana (BEE-SME case study). Low material loss.

alloy_steel_turning.notes: Higher cutting forces and tool wear for alloy grades. More passes required, increasing SEC.

alloy_steel_milling.notes: Difficult-to-machine alloy steels require slower feeds, more coolant, higher energy.

stainless_steel_turning.notes: Work hardening tendency of stainless increases cutting energy significantly. Tool changes increase downtime and indirect energy.

aluminium_turning.notes: Aluminium machines at 3-5x higher speeds than steel. Very low SEC. Common in auto component clusters.

aluminium_milling.notes: High-speed milling of aluminium components. Low cutting forces and power consumption.

cast_iron_turning.notes: Dry machining common for grey cast iron (no coolant needed). Moderate SEC. Common in foundry-machining integrated MSME units (Coimbatore, Rajkot).

cast_iron_milling.notes: Face milling of cast iron housings, blocks. Higher SEC than turning due to chip formation energy.

brass_turning.notes: Brass is free-cutting — very low SEC. Jamnagar brass cluster produces precision turned parts. Chips have high scrap value.

emission_factors._source: IPCC AR5 / World Steel Association / International Aluminium Institute

grid_emission_factor._source: CEA CO2 Baseline Database for Indian Power Sector, Version 18 (2023-24)
